channel.title: iDigital
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from iDigital, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Flipping the script: How ‘upside-down’ AutoPallet robots solve palletizing density
item.description: AutoPallet debuts magnetic, ceiling-mounted AMRs at Manifest 2026, providing high-density palletizing through a suspended swarm architecture. The post...

item.title: The Download: what Moltbook tells us about AI hype, and the rise and rise of AI therapy
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Moltbook was...

item.title: 11 reasons robots struggle to scale in high-mix manufacturing
item.description: Few robotics technologies have been actually deployed in high-mix applications. Let's explore the reasons behind this slow transition. The post 11 rea...

item.title: Making AI Work, MIT Technology Review’s new AI newsletter, is here
item.description: For years, our newsroom has explored AI’s limitations and potential dangers, as well as its growing energy needs. And our reporters have looked closel...

item.title: Why the Moltbook frenzy was like Pokémon
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Lots of infl...

item.title: What the SpaceX acquisition of xAI means for industrial robotics
item.description: The consolidation of SpaceX and xAI could lead to more adaptive use of robots, data, and AI in manufacturing, says Flexxbotics' CEO. The post What the...

item.title: KinetIQ framework from Humanoid orchestrates robot fleets
item.description: Humanoid has introduced KinetIQ, an AI framework designed to orchestrate robot fleets across industrial and service applications. The post KinetIQ fra...

item.title: North American robot orders rise by 6.6% in 2025, reports A3
item.description: In 2025, North American robot orders from non-automotive customers led the way, with cobots gaining more traction as well. The post North American rob...

item.title: The Download: helping cancer survivors to give birth, and cleaning up Bangladesh’s garment industry
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. An experimen...

item.title: Moltbook was peak AI theater
item.description: For a few days this week the hottest new hangout on the internet was a vibe-coded Reddit clone called Moltbook, which billed itself as a social networ...

item.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
item.description: Railway , a San Francisco-based cloud platform that has quietly amassed two million developers without spending a dollar on marketing, announced Thurs...

item.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
item.description: The artificial intelligence coding revolution comes with a catch: it's expensive. Claude Code , Anthropic's terminal-based AI agent that can write, de...

item.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
item.description: Alfred Wahlforss was running out of options. His startup, Listen Labs , needed to hire over 100 engineers, but competing against Mark Zuckerberg's $10...

item.title: Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI
item.description: Salesforce on Tuesday launched an entirely rebuilt version of Slackbot , the company's workplace assistant, transforming it from a simple notification...

item.title: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
item.description: Anthropic released Cowork on Monday, a new AI agent capability that extends the power of its wildly successful Claude Code tool to non-technical users...

item.title: Promise and Perils of Using AI for Hiring: Guard Against Data Bias
item.description: By AI Trends Staff While AI in hiring is now widely used for writing job descriptions, screening candidates, and automating interviews, it poses a ris...

item.title: Best Practices for Building the AI Development Platform in Government
item.description: By John P. Desmond, AI Trends Editor The AI stack defined by Carnegie Mellon University is fundamental to the approach being taken by the US Army for ...

item.title: Advance Trustworthy AI and ML, and Identify Best Practices for Scaling AI
item.description: By John P. Desmond, AI Trends Editor Advancing trustworthy AI and machine learning to mitigate agency risk is a priority for the US Department of Ener...

item.title: Predictive Maintenance Proving Out as Successful AI Use Case
item.description: By John P. Desmond, AI Trends Editor More companies are successfully exploiting predictive maintenance systems that combine AI and IoT sensors to coll...

item.title: Novelty In The Game Of Go Provides Bright Insights For AI And Autonomous Vehicles
item.description: By Lance Eliot, the AI Trends Insider We already expect that humans to exhibit flashes of brilliance. It might not happen all the time, but the act it...
